title: Cato Security Event Monitoring (Cato Networks)
dataSources: Cato Events API (security, connectivity, audit); `sourcetype=cato:events` or `sourcetype=cato:sase`
app: Cato Networks Events App (Splunkbase 8037); `eventsFeed.py` from [catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration](https://github.com/catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration) (syslog to TCP 1514) as an alternative to the app’s API pull.
spl:
index=sase sourcetype IN ("cato:events","cato:sase") earliest=-24h
| eval sub_type=lower(coalesce(event_sub_type, sub_type, ""))
| eval threat_cat=coalesce(threat_category, rule_category, signature_category, "uncategorized")
| eval sev=coalesce(severity, priority, risk_level, "info")
| where match(sub_type,"ips|anti.?malware|malware|intrusion|ngfw|firewall|threat") OR match(lower(coalesce(action, disposition, "")),"block|prevent|deny|detect")
| timechart span=1h count by sev
description: Cato's cloud-native security stack generates IPS, anti-malware, and NGFW events from a single pass inspection of all WAN and internet traffic. Unlike on-premises firewalls, every branch and remote user traverses the same cloud inspection plane. Monitoring detection volume, severity distribution, and threat categories across sites and identities reveals coordinated campaigns, noisy rules, and coverage gaps before incidents escalate.
value: Security operations teams monitor all Cato Networks security events (IPS, Anti-Malware, Firewall, URL Filtering) through a unified pipeline, leveraging Cato's single-vendor SASE architecture for streamlined threat detection and cross-service correlation.
implementation: Install the Cato Networks Events App and map Cato JSON fields with `props.conf` / `FIELDALIAS` to stable names (`event_sub_type`, `threat_category`, `site_name`, `user`). Baseline events per hour per site; alert on 3σ spikes in blocked or critical-severity counts. Tag change windows to suppress expected noise after policy rollouts.
visualization: Timechart (events/hour by severity), Pie or bar (threat category mix), Table (top signatures/rules), Single value (24h blocked vs prior day).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cato Networks Events API to Splunk. Data in `index=cato` with `sourcetype=cato:events`. Cato provides events via its Events API (REST) or syslog CEF format. Key fields: `event_type` (security/connectivity/system), `event_sub_type` (IPS/Anti-Malware/Firewall/URL Filtering), `action` (Allow/Block/Monitor), `src_ip`, `dst_ip`, `user_name`, `rule_name`, `threat_name`, `severity`, `risk_level`.
• Cato Networks is a single-vendor SASE platform that converges SD-WAN, FWaaS, SWG, CASB, and ZTNA. All security events flow through a single event pipeline, making correlation straightforward.
• Cato Events API: Cato Management Application > Events > API Integration > generate API key. Configure a scripted input to poll the Events API.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify Cato event flow:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" earliest=-1h
| stats count by event_type, event_sub_type
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Security event overview:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" event_type=security earliest=-24h
| stats count dc(src_ip) as sources dc(dst_ip) as targets by event_sub_type, action, severity
| sort severity, - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Provides a unified view of all Cato security events by service type (IPS, Anti-Malware, Firewall, URL Filtering). Cato's advantage is that all events share the same schema, so a single search covers the entire security stack. High-severity IPS blocks with many sources indicate an active attack. Anti-Malware blocks indicate users downloading malicious files. URL Filtering blocks indicate policy enforcement.

Threat landscape:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" event_type=security severity IN ("High","Critical") earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(src_ip) as sources values(threat_name) as threats by event_sub_type
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Cato Management: Monitoring > Events > Security. Compare counts.
(b) Trigger a test IPS event (Nmap scan) and verify it appears.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Security event volume by service. Row 2 — Threat severity distribution. Row 3 — Top threats.
Alert: Immediate on Critical severity events.

Runbook:
1. **IPS Critical** — Active exploitation attempt. Verify source and block.
2. **Anti-Malware block** — User downloading malware. Check user's activity.
3. **URL Filtering spike** — New category or active phishing campaign.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No Cato events** — Events API key may have expired. Regenerate in Cato Management.
• **Only connectivity events** — Security event types may not be enabled in the API filter.
grandmaExplanation: Cato Networks runs all our security services in one cloud platform. We monitor every security event — blocked attacks, caught malware, filtered websites — in one place, which makes it much easier to see the full picture of what threats we face.
hardware: Cato Socket (physical edge), Cato vSocket (virtual edge), Cato SDP Client (ZTNA endpoint software) — no discrete on-premises firewall appliances; enforcement is cloud-delivered at Cato PoPs.
